living-concrete.knowledge: Bare repo = git database, no working directory. Create: git clone --bare URL PATH Contains: objects/, refs/, HEAD, config Use: local origin, worktree source, mirror
Pattern: REMOTE -> BARE -> WORKTREE Bare = "local GitHub" - single source of truth. Multiple worktrees share one object database.

Worktree = additional working dir linked to repo. Create: git worktree add PATH REF Each: own HEAD, index, working files. Shared: objects, refs, remote config. List: git worktree list | Remove: git worktree remove PATH
git -C BARE worktree add PATH BRANCH Path: relative to bare or absolute. Branch must exist OR use -b to create.
living-concrete.pattern: One worktree per branch (git-enforced). Use different branches for parallel work.
living-concrete.knowledge: Submodule = pointer to commit in another repo. .gitmodules defines URL mappings. Parent tracks commit SHA, not branch. Init: git submodule update --init (ONE level) Recursive: git submodule update --init --recursive (ALL)
living-concrete.pattern: BentOS: ONE LEVEL ONLY (direct submodules = worlds). Use --init without --recursive. World internal submodules = implementation detail.
living-concrete.knowledge: Refs = human names -> commits. branches: refs/heads/* | tags: refs/tags/* remotes: refs/remotes/* | HEAD: current checkout
Push: git -C WORKTREE push origin BRANCH (worktree->bare->remote) Fetch: git -C BARE fetch origin (updates bare refs) Pull: git -C WORKTREE pull origin BRANCH
